results.question: Show the stadium name and capacity with most number of concerts in year 2014 or after.
results.gold_sql: SELECT T2.name ,  T2.capacity FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.stadium_id  =  T2.stadium_id WHERE T1.year  >=  2014 GROUP BY T2.stadium_id ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT s.Name, s.Capacity
FROM stadium s
JOIN concert c ON s.Stadium_ID = c.Stadium_ID
WHERE c.Year >= '2014'
GROUP BY s.Stadium_ID
ORDER BY COUNT(c.concert_ID) DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: What is the name and capacity of the stadium with the most concerts after 2013 ?
results.gold_sql: select t2.name ,  t2.capacity from concert as t1 join stadium as t2 on t1.stadium_id  =  t2.stadium_id where t1.year  >  2013 group by t2.stadium_id order by count(*) desc limit 1
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT s.Name, s.Capacity
FROM stadium s
JOIN concert c ON s.Stadium_ID = c.Stadium_ID
WHERE c.Year > '2013'
GROUP BY s.Stadium_ID
ORDER BY COUNT(c.concert_ID) DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: What is the average, minimum, and maximum age of all singers from France?
results.gold_sql: SELECT avg(age) ,  min(age) ,  max(age) FROM singer WHERE country  =  'France'

results.question: Which year has most number of concerts?
results.gold_sql: SELECT YEAR FROM concert GROUP BY YEAR ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT Year, COUNT(*) AS concert_count
FROM concert
GROUP BY Year
ORDER BY concert_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

results.question: Find the number of concerts happened in the stadium with the highest capacity .
results.gold_sql: select count(*) from concert where stadium_id = (select stadium_id from stadium order by capacity desc limit 1)

results.question: Show the stadium name and the number of concerts in each stadium.
results.gold_sql: SELECT T2.name ,  count(*) FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.stadium_id  =  T2.stadium_id GROUP BY T1.stadium_id
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT s.Name, COUNT(c.concert_ID) AS Number_of_Concerts
FROM stadium s
LEFT JOIN concert c ON s.Stadium_ID = c.Stadium_ID
GROUP BY s.Name;

results.question: Show the name and theme for all concerts and the number of singers in each concert.
results.gold_sql: SELECT T2.concert_name ,  T2.theme ,  count(*) FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN concert AS T2 ON T1.concert_id  =  T2.concert_id GROUP BY T2.concert_id
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT c.concert_Name, c.Theme, COUNT(sic.Singer_ID) AS Number_of_Singers
FROM concert c
LEFT JOIN singer_in_concert sic ON c.concert_ID = sic.concert_ID
GROUP BY c.concert_ID;

results.question: List singer names and number of concerts for each singer.
results.gold_sql: SELECT T2.name ,  count(*) FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.singer_id  =  T2.singer_id GROUP BY T2.singer_id
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT s.Name, COUNT(sc.concert_ID) AS Number_of_Concerts
FROM singer s
LEFT JOIN singer_in_concert sc ON s.Singer_ID = sc.Singer_ID
GROUP BY s.Singer_ID;

results.question: Show the name and the release year of the song by the youngest singer.
results.gold_sql: SELECT song_name ,  song_release_year FROM singer ORDER BY age LIMIT 1

results.question: Show all countries and the number of singers in each country.
results.gold_sql: SELECT country ,  count(*) FROM singer GROUP BY country

results.question: Find number of pets owned by students who are older than 20.
results.gold_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid WHERE T1.age  >  20

results.question: Find the number of dog pets that are raised by female students (with sex F).
results.gold_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T2.petid  =  T3.petid WHERE T1.sex  =  'F' AND T3.pettype  =  'dog'
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM Has_Pet HP 
JOIN Student S ON HP.StuID = S.StuID 
JOIN Pets P ON HP.PetID = P.PetID 
WHERE S.Sex = 'F' AND P.PetType = 'dog';

results.question: Find the average and maximum age for each type of pet.
results.gold_sql: SELECT avg(pet_age) ,  max(pet_age) ,  pettype FROM pets GROUP BY pettype

results.question: Find the number of pets for each student who has any pet and student id.
results.gold_sql: SELECT count(*) ,  T1.stuid FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid GROUP BY T1.stuid

results.question: Find the first name and gender of student who have more than one pet.
results.gold_sql: SELECT T1.fname ,  T1.sex FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid GROUP BY T1.stuid HAVING count(*)  >  1
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT S.Fname, S.Sex
FROM Student S
JOIN Has_Pet HP ON S.StuID = HP.StuID
GROUP BY S.StuID
HAVING COUNT(HP.PetID) > 1;

results.question: Find the number of pets whose weight is heavier than 10.
results.gold_sql: SELECT count(*) FROM pets WHERE weight  >  10
results.predicted_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Pets WHERE weight > 10;

results.question: Find the weight of the youngest dog.
results.gold_sql: SELECT weight FROM pets ORDER BY pet_age LIMIT 1
results.predicted_sql: SELECT weight FROM Pets WHERE PetType = 'dog' ORDER BY pet_age ASC LIMIT 1;

results.question: Find the maximum weight for each type of pet. List the maximum weight and pet type.
results.gold_sql: SELECT max(weight) ,  petType FROM pets GROUP BY petType

results.question: Find the first name of students who have cat or dog pet.
results.gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pettype  =  'cat' OR T3.pettype  =  'dog'
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT Student.Fname 
FROM Student 
JOIN Has_Pet ON Student.StuID = Has_Pet.StuID 
JOIN Pets ON Has_Pet.PetID = Pets.PetID 
WHERE Pets.PetType IN ('cat', 'dog');

results.question: Find the first name of students who have both cat and dog pets .
results.gold_sql: select t1.fname from student as t1 join has_pet as t2 on t1.stuid  =  t2.stuid join pets as t3 on t3.petid  =  t2.petid where t3.pettype  =  'cat' intersect select t1.fname from student as t1 join has_pet as t2 on t1.stuid  =  t2.stuid join pets as t3 on t3.petid  =  t2.petid where t3.pettype  =  'dog'
results.predicted_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT s.Fname
FROM Student s
JOIN Has_Pet hp1 ON s.StuID = hp1.StuID
JOIN Pets p1 ON hp1.PetID = p1.PetID AND p1.PetType = 'cat'
JOIN Has_Pet hp2 ON s.StuID = hp2.StuID
JOIN Pets p2 ON hp2.PetID = p2.PetID AND p2.PetType = 'dog';
